compatibility.contract: sync-native HTTP, SQLite transactions, wake, permissions, query lifecycle
sync-wasm representation and the local Durable Object host boundary
Named-query results and randomized incremental behavior against Zero 1.7.0

suites.name: Deterministic Rust core
suites.whatItProves: This suite covers cookies, patches, mutation IDs, retention, query membership, permissions, and model invariants at the recorded SHA.
suites.whatItDoesNotProve: It does not prove HTTP host lifecycle, workerd behavior, production capacity, or application-specific schemas and policies.

suites.name: Native host qualification
suites.operationUnit: distinct native-host conformance lanes; per-lane totals (sweep rounds, storm clients, query shapes) are not summed into an operation count
suites.whatItProves: This suite exercises the release native binary through real Zero clients, concurrent clients, permissions, wake, and query lifecycle; validates recorded atomic-visibility and exactly-once histories with checkers that are themselves red-proofed; runs the pinned Elle list-append analysis on the recorded history; and cuts a mutation's row effects from its ack under a one-row diff cap.
suites.whatItDoesNotProve: It does not prove the Cloudflare host, multi-region behavior, unbounded namespace sizes, or a consumer's recovery objectives.

suites.name: WASM / workerd qualification
suites.operationUnit: distinct sync-cf-host lanes; the operation count is recorded state-machine lifecycle steps, not push-soak or protocol-fuzz totals
suites.whatItProves: This suite covers WASM value boundaries, local Durable Object SQLite transactions, ingest, restart, push-memory soak, protocol fuzz, recovery faults, backup/restore, and generated lifecycle transitions under the pinned workerd.
suites.whatItDoesNotProve: Local workerd is not the Cloudflare production service and does not establish isolate memory, regional propagation, quota, or eviction behavior in a deployed account.

suites.name: Native fault and lifecycle qualification
suites.operationUnit: distinct fault and lifecycle lanes; the operation count is recorded state-machine lifecycle steps, not protocol-fuzz cases
suites.whatItProves: This suite covers pinned upstream corpus agreement, malformed protocol input, reconnect/retention (including a client offline past the retention floor), process eviction, transactional storage faults, backup/restore, and the composed nemesis state machine on the native host: receipted engine faults at named boundaries, a transport outage held open across a fault arm and a restart, and a full prune plus restart that must keep the served cookie monotonic.
suites.whatItDoesNotProve: Injected faults cover named boundaries and generated traces, not every operating-system, filesystem, hardware, or application failure mode.

suites.name: Stock-Zero differential
suites.operationUnit: distinct stock-Zero differential harness lanes; per-query comparisons are not summed into an operation total
suites.whatItProves: Named and seeded query scenarios produce the same observable results as the pinned stock Zero server for the exercised corpus.
suites.whatItDoesNotProve: Differential agreement is limited to the recorded corpus and Zero version; it is not a claim of internal implementation equivalence or future-version compatibility.

knownLimitations: The checked-in fallback contains no exact-SHA CI result. A qualified main or tag build replaces it during the site build.
The workerd lane is local emulation; deployed Cloudflare isolate memory, quota, eviction, and regional propagation require a separate named deployment qualification.
The compatibility corpus is pinned to Zero 1.7.0 and does not imply compatibility with later Zero releases.
GitHub Actions logs and artifacts are immutable per run but remain subject to the repository's retention policy.

unresolvedLanes.name: Exact-SHA CI evidence
unresolvedLanes.detail: The repository fallback intentionally contains no generated workflow result.

unresolvedLanes.name: Deployed Cloudflare qualification
unresolvedLanes.detail: The deployed rust-cf query differential and platform-specific soak remain outside the standard CI gate.

gate.policy: CI publishes verified build evidence only after every required job succeeds at the exact build SHA. It labels that build a verified release only when the immutable release tag resolves to the same SHA.
